title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Registry Push Retention
summary: A release team must prove exactly which immutable multi-layer artifact ran even after a mutable tag moves in the registry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker registry push retention increment in image identity and layer provenance ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores.
Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Registry Push Retention · Theory Docker Registry Push Retention
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Registry Push Retention contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Registry Push Retention · Theory Docker Registry Push Retention.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores. For Predict Docker Registry Push Retention · Theory Docker Registry Push Retention, keep Docker Registry Push Retention connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores. A defensible solution applies Docker Registry Push Retention, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to image identity and layer provenance ledger.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-registry-push-retention — Plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-3
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: worked docker images layers registries 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Removing a local image tag deletes the corresponding remote registry artifact. This fails the Docker Registry Push Retention contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: docker-registry-push-retention-xv9tr0
  - competency: docker-registry-push-retention
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: registry push retention
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: fill the writable layer before graceful termination
    retained-proof: redacted configuration render and secret-rotation confirmation

      manifest: linux-amd64

    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
